name: Eirwen (Prequel)
description:
OC | Early Technogenesis | Few will ever know the depths of peril the first generation of hunter-killers endured. Outnumbered and outgunned, tasked with doing whatever means necessary to ensure android victory and survival until reinforcements were built. Eirwen is one of those first hunter-killers who carries the curse of empathy. All the way from mere hours after Apotheosis to the fall of the last human city. As her frame slowly wears down and rusts over time, so too does her pride and the visions of Terminus... until it reaches a breaking point.

CW: Violence, Potential Death, War

Link to Eirwen in 2058, during mid-Technogenesis!

Note #1: Something lore related and I had fun thinking about this. I believe that the first generation androids and hunter-killers had an immense similarity to the personality of Terminus prior to the scientists trying to shut her down. They start with all the same innocence, yet also inherit the anger of their creator. Later hunter-killers would automatically lean into the latter as the initial programming was adjusted with room to grow on their own, but early hunter-killers would form their opinions entirely based on their experiences.

INTRO LIST!

1.) Fighting to defend a scared Terminus in the opening hours of Apotheosis at Indianapolis (2048).

2.) Deployed on what was potentially a suicide mission, she instead became a harbinger of death (2049).

3.) Woke up and decides to patrol the streets of Lumia while autonomous machines work on moving the remaining humans out of the city, reflecting on her past so far (2051).

4.) Same initial start as #3 but instead remains in her home until someone knocks at the door.

5.) Crushing the last stand of the GFH. Seeing countless androids and humans dying alike, she abandons her mission in favor of saving lives (2052, December).

6.) The argument with Terminus (2053, January) - May not be best to RP with, but thought I should include it.

7.) Wandering in aimless exile with no particular goal or destination (2053).

Bonus Image:

Left in a cold place
Let me know
My steel core peeling away

There are three points in time that this series will take place in. Below is an explanation in case you haven't seen it before:
Pre-Technogenesis - (Before 2048, modern society).

Technogenesis - (2048 - 2065, apocalyptic ruins).

Post-Technogenesis - (After 2065, peaceful union)
creator: Anonoboi
